title: RAN Degradation — Edinburgh South Cell Cluster Throughput Loss

affected_services.name: 4G Mobile Broadband — Edinburgh South Cluster
affected_services.description: Downlink throughput reduced by approximately 60% across 12 cell sites in Edinburgh South cluster. Average user throughput fallen from 45 Mbps to 18 Mbps. Packet loss elevated to 3.2%.

affected_services.name: 5G NR — Edinburgh South Cluster
affected_services.description: 5G NSA anchor band (Band 1) affected. 5G users experiencing fallback to degraded 4G. Average 5G throughput fallen from 180 Mbps to 55 Mbps.

affected_services.name: VoLTE — Edinburgh South Cells
affected_services.description: VoLTE MOS scores slightly degraded in affected area. Occasional call setup delays of 2-3 seconds. No call drops reported.

estimated_customer_impact.geographic_area: Edinburgh South — Morningside, Marchmont, Bruntsfield, Tollcross

root_cause_hypothesis: Suspected CPRI link degradation between BBU pool at Morningside Exchange and remote radio heads at multiple sites. Possibly related to overnight fibre maintenance by Openreach in the Morningside area. Investigating whether fibre patch panel disturbance has introduced excess attenuation on CPRI fronthaul links.

timeline.event: Automated RAN performance alert — Edinburgh South cluster throughput anomaly detected by SON platform
12 cell sites flagged with downlink throughput KPI below threshold (< 25 Mbps average)
NOC Tier 1 operator acknowledged alert cluster. Incident VF-INC-2026-00923 created.
Escalated to Tier 2 RAN Operations. BBU and RRH alarm review initiated.
Tier 2 identified CPRI link errors on 8 of 12 affected sites. All sites served from Morningside BBU pool.
Openreach overnight works ticket identified: fibre maintenance at Morningside Exchange, completed 06:00. Possible fronthaul fibre disturbance.
Field engineer dispatched to Morningside Exchange to inspect CPRI fronthaul fibre patch panel.
Engineer on site. Visual inspection of patch panel — two CPRI fibre patches appear to have been reseated incorrectly during Openreach maintenance.

escalation_history.reason: Multiple cell site performance alerts — cluster pattern identified

notes: Pattern consistent with CPRI fibre disturbance at BBU pool site. Openreach maintenance overnight may have inadvertently moved CPRI patch fibres while working on adjacent infrastructure. Engineer on site performing fibre re-patching and CPRI link verification. Expected resolution within 2 hours of engineer arrival. Formal complaint to Openreach to be raised via Access Services team regarding third-party damage to Vodafone infrastructure.
